summary: The conjecture that every $k$-regular directed graph with no parallel arcs contains $\binom{k+1}{2}$ arc-disjoint directed cycles remains open. The best known lower bound continues to be Alon's 1996 result of $\frac{1}{128}k^2$ arc-disjoint directed cycles in any digraph with minimum outdegree at least $k$, and no post-2013 paper improving this constant or proving the conjecture was found.
search_queries: arc-disjoint directed cycles regular directed graphs conjecture Alon McDiarmid Molloy
arc-disjoint cycles digraph minimum outdegree k^2 bound improvement

"edge-disjoint" OR "arc-disjoint" cycles "k-regular directed" OR "regular directed graph" Alon conjecture improve bound arxiv 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
"disjoint directed cycles" "k-regular" OR "regular digraph" improved constant coefficient quadratic 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025 2026
notes: All six search queries were exhausted without finding a post-2013 paper specifically improving the arc-disjoint cycles bound in k-regular digraphs. The arXiv paper 1701.06364 (Bucić, 2018) improves the constant for vertex-disjoint cycles under minimum outdegree conditions (reducing 64k to 18k for guaranteeing k vertex-disjoint cycles), but addresses a different problem. The 2026 paper 2604.13700 (Steiner) concerns openly disjoint cycles and directed tree-width in regular digraphs, not arc-disjoint cycles. The ScienceDirect page for the 2020 Discrete Mathematics paper on disjoint directed cycles returned HTTP 403. Confidence is medium rather than high because the ScienceDirect paper (pii/S0012365X20301199) could not be verified and might contain relevant partial results.
